content:
- Executed meta-task 18 pass-5 (deep_review) per NOW EXECUTE plan; evidence dir: .ralph/evidence/meta-18-pass5-20260304T122649Z.
- Preflight: verified required real binaries + tooling; captured versions/identity/hashes; checked /tmp space.
- Gate realism hardening: Makefile now forbids external ULTRA_LONG_TESTS overrides, removes ALLOW_EMPTY zero-test pass, and adds cargo --list preflight collision checks for --skip tokens.
- Tool installers: install-postgres16.sh no longer trusts PATH hits (trusted absolute candidates + per-binary major=16 validation + realpath logging); install-etcd.sh now enforces pinned SHA256 + strict version format + atomic install + version string check.
- DCS correctness: etcd store now clears pending watch-event queue on session break (disconnect-gap stale apply fix) via centralized invalidation helper; tightened reconnect tests; added real-etcd regression test.
- HA e2e strictness: removed best-effort integrity skip path in no-quorum fencing; scenario matrix uses strict all-node fail-safe; partition chaos stable primary now requires full observability; split-brain window check fails closed when zero samples.
- Provenance: ran strace execve proof showing .tools/** binaries executed; ran negative control etcd stub with marker + expected failure (restored via trap).
- Ran full gates 100% green: make check/test/lint/test-long.
- Closeout: set .ralph/model.txt back to normal_high; ran .ralph/task_switch.sh; committed + pushed.
- Surprise: git post-commit hook ran docs publish (mdbook build + commit/push to pgtuskmaster-docs repo) automatically during commits.
- Next: none; pass-5 complete and pushed.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and searched for failing tasks (<passes>false</passes>/<passing>false</passing>) and meta-tasks.
- Found no failing tasks, but found a recurring meta-task (<passes>meta-task</passes>): .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md
- Next: select that meta-task by writing its path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- User reported live control-loop diagram not rendering on Cloudflare URL.
- Investigated generated HTML and found Mermaid blocks present but no mermaid runtime scripts included.
- Ran `mdbook-mermaid install docs` which added docs/mermaid.min.js, docs/mermaid-init.js, and book.toml output.html.additional-js entries.
- Publish run temporarily regressed to dist/out due script file currently being old variant; detected from publish output and script content.
- Re-patched scripts/publish-docs.sh back to dist root mode and reran publish.
- Latest docs repo push: 77224de4; dist now includes mermaid.min.js + mermaid-init.js and control-loop page includes script tags for both.
- wrangler.jsonc confirms assets.directory is ./dist.

- Inspected DCS watch semantics across src/dcs/store.rs, src/dcs/worker.rs, src/dcs/etcd_store.rs, src/dcs/keys.rs.
- Confirmed reconnect flow: invalidate_watch_session clears queue and marks unhealthy; reconnect bootstrap emits synthetic Reset + snapshot PUTs and replaces queue on reconnect.
- Mapped existing tests for reset semantics: store unit tests and real-etcd reconnect tests (empty snapshot, non-empty authoritative snapshot, disconnect clears queue before reset).
- Noted skeptical gaps: no explicit test for compaction/canceled watch triggering reset path; no assertion of revision monotonicity / ignoring stale revision events; no direct assertion that full snapshot batch is delivered atomically to consumer beyond ordering assumptions.
- Next if needed: implement/add targeted tests for compaction-triggered reconnect, revision regression defense, and reset-path scope validation.

- Reviewed tools/install-postgres16.sh, tools/install-etcd.sh, src/test_harness/binaries.rs plus related runtime execution paths.
- Confirmed strong points: etcd installer has strict version allowlist + SHA-256 pinning per arch and verifies both cached/new archives before install; harness resolves binaries by explicit repository-anchored paths and validates file/executable status before use.
- Noted weak spots for deep review: postgres installer relies on dnf trust chain (no explicit package checksum/version pin), PATH trust for installer helper commands, /usr/bin fallback can accept pre-existing local binaries if version string matches 16.x, harness/config path validators only enforce non-empty path (no absolute/canonical/ownership checks), no runtime version/hash attestation when binaries are executed.
- Also traced runtime process worker: process.binaries paths are used directly in Command::new and only validated as non-empty, so config-fed relative/symlink paths are in trust boundary for non-test runtime.
- Next if needed: propose concrete hardening patch set (absolute command paths in installers, rpm NEVRA pinning, canonical path + ownership/mode validation, runtime --version attestation for binaries).

- Ran required progress_read startup command and then performed broad smell census with rg.
- Verified zero direct uses of unwrap/expect/panic!/todo!/unimplemented! and no clippy allow/ignore test attrs in Rust source/test files.
- Identified major leniency hotspots around best-effort flows and swallowed errors: src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs, src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs, src/test_harness/ports.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs, src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs, src/runtime/node.rs.
- Collected supporting scans for let _ =, .ok(), if let Err + continue, timeout/retry/fallback logic, and Makefile test gating behavior.
- Next step if continuing: do deep skeptical read in prioritized modules and classify each best-effort/ignored-error path as acceptable test hygiene vs production risk, then propose concrete hardening patches.

- Scanned Makefile gate targets: check/test/test-long/lint, plus directly invoked script tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh.
- Verified make test fail-closed behavior: preflight cargo test -- --list, exact token existence checks, duplicate/ambiguity checks, global timeout wrapper, and explicit RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 rejection.
- Verified make test-long behavior: non-empty ULTRA_LONG_TESTS guard, exact-match preflight and --exact execution per ultra-long test.
- Audited real-binary enforcement path in src/test_harness/binaries.rs and ha_e2e startup: .tools/postgres16/bin + .tools/etcd/bin are hard prerequisites (missing binaries produce errors, not skips).
- Confirmed no #[ignore] tests in src/tests/examples.
- Noted actionable risks: (1) docs-lint scope only checks docs/src/{architecture,concepts,interfaces,testing}; docs elsewhere bypasses architecture no-code guard. (2) make test-long has no outer timeout; one ULTRA_LONG test (e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity) lacks per-test outer tokio timeout wrapper, so hangs can run indefinitely in test-long.
- Also noted operational gotcha: make test 120s timeout includes compilation, can false-fail on cold builds/slow hosts.
- Next: hand user concise checklist with file/line references and suggested hardening actions.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` (recurring meta deep-review task).
- Verified `.ralph/model.txt` was `normal_high` (mismatch for this meta-task). Wrote a new pass-6 plan into the task file and ended it with `TO BE VERIFIED` per new workflow.
- Spawned explorer subagents to map: Makefile gate behavior/timeout/silent-pass risks, installer + real-binary harness posture, repo-wide smell scan hotspots, and etcd watch reconnect semantics + test gaps.
- Appended consolidated pass-6 plan (covers: scans, gate realism, real pg16/etcd installs, skeptical deep reads, bug/task creation, and mandatory `make check/test/lint/test-long`).
- Set `.ralph/model.txt` to `deep_review` so the next run can start immediately on the correct model.

- Next: re-open the task file; since it ends with `TO BE VERIFIED`, do a DEEP skeptical verification (use subagents) and alter at least one plan item; then replace the marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- After `NOW EXECUTE`: execute the plan strictly, tick boxes, create bug/task files for findings, and only then run all gates and perform the commit/push + task switch.

- Implemented Mermaid docs linting to catch diagram syntax errors pre-publish without introducing new network dependencies.
- Added tools/docs-mermaid-lint.mjs:
  - Recursively scans docs/src/**/*.md.
  - Extracts ```mermaid fenced blocks with file/line tracking.
  - Loads repo-vendored docs/mermaid.min.js in Node and parses each block.
  - Fails closed on parse errors and unclosed mermaid fences.
  - Added a Node compatibility shim for Mermaid bundle DOMPurify method expectations (`addHook`/`sanitize`) so parser works headlessly in this environment.
- Wired lint into Makefile:
  - Added ensure-node target.
  - Updated docs-lint to run Mermaid lint before existing no-code-guard.
- Wired publish safety:
  - Updated scripts/publish-docs.sh to run `make docs-lint` before mdBook build.
- While validating, Mermaid lint exposed real diagram issues; fixed syntax in:
  - docs/src/concepts/mental-model.md
  - docs/src/architecture/safety-and-fencing.md
  - docs/src/architecture/system-context.md
  - docs/src/operations/docs.md
- Verification run results:
  - `node ./tools/docs-mermaid-lint.mjs` passes.
  - `make docs-lint` passes.
  - `make docs-build` passes.
- Notes:
  - mdBook warns that mdbook-mermaid was built against 0.4.36 while mdBook is 0.4.40; this pre-existing warning remains non-fatal.
- Next if asked:
  - Optionally add this check to CI workflow explicitly if not already running `make lint` or `make docs-lint`.

- User asked to commit the docs-lint/publish changes.
- Read progress log at startup, confirmed unrelated .ralph working-memory files were dirty and intentionally left untouched.
- Staged only the docs-lint/publish patchset:
  Makefile, scripts/publish-docs.sh, tools/docs-mermaid-lint.mjs, docs/book.toml, docs/mermaid-init.js, docs/mermaid.min.js,
  docs/src/concepts/mental-model.md, docs/src/architecture/safety-and-fencing.md,
  docs/src/architecture/system-context.md, docs/src/operations/docs.md.
- Created commit in this repo:
  60114c2 "docs: lint mermaid diagrams before build and publish"
- Post-commit hook executed automatically and succeeded:
  - ran docs-lint (including new Mermaid parser lint)
  - built mdBook successfully
  - synced/pushed docs repo commit 4d67b635 to djosh34/pgtuskmaster-docs main
- Current working tree still has only unrelated .ralph modifications/untracked files.

- Task: user requested complete docs review for quality/readability/structure and noise/irrelevance.
- Actions: read all docs files in docs/src (including verification report) and AGENTS/RUST_SYSTEM_HARNESS_PLAN.
- Findings: docs are technically strong and internally consistent; good diagrams + evidence-oriented rigor, but weak onboarding for complete first-time users due to missing hands-on quickstart, setup walkthrough, and troubleshooting.
- Key issues: top-level includes maintainer-oriented pages (docs-style, audit report) that can feel out-of-place for newcomers; some redirect stubs remain in tree.
- Next if continuing: add a concise getting-started/setup page and move or hide non-user documentation behind contribution docs so new-user path stays clean.

- Read all docs pages in docs/src (summary, intro, concepts, architecture, interfaces, operations, testing, verification, and redirect stubs) plus top-level AGENTS.md and RUST_SYSTEM_HARNESS_PLAN.md.
- User asked for very honest doc quality assessment focused on newcomer clarity, ordering, structure, language flow, and unnecessary process/rules content.
- Conclusion: strong architecture explanation and good consistency/diagram quality, but onboarding flow is weakened by process-heavy pages in main nav (Docs Style + Verification report), redirect-stub clutter, and occasional internal terminology density for first-time readers.
- Next if continuing: propose concrete docs IA cleanup (split user docs vs contributor/meta docs, move verification/docs-style out of default nav, remove redirect stubs, add quickstart/tutorial path) and optionally implement patch.

- User asked for proposed docs reorganization, writing-style adjustment in assistant output, intensive multi-source research on documentation writing, and creation of an agent task via add-task-as-agent.
- Used add-task-as-agent skill instructions and created new task file in same story dir:
  .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/42-task-reorganize-docs-information-architecture-and-writing-quality.md
- Researched documentation/writing guidance from multiple source types: Google developer style + tech writing, Microsoft style, Write the Docs principles, Diataxis/Divio framework, GOV.UK plain language guidance, NARA plain-language principles, and Purdue OWL cohesion guidance.
- Next if continuing: implement task 42 by restructuring SUMMARY, adding getting-started section, moving contributor/meta docs out of newcomer flow, and rewriting chapter openings/transitions.

- User asked to adjust task direction: chapters can be merged/extended, add more why/tradeoffs for operator audience, avoid legacy framing language, and move task into a new story.
- Created new story dir .ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs and moved/replaced previous docs IA task with:
  01-task-restructure-operator-docs-for-flow-depth-and-rationale.md
- New task now explicitly requires: merging short chapters, expanding depth, adding Why/Tradeoffs/Operational impact sections, removing decorative TOC diagrams, and keeping contributor docs out of default operator path.
- Deleted old task file from story-rust-system-harness.

- User requested two additions: enforce no code in docs except config docs with detailed field meaning/behavior, and add a separate post-transformation deep skeptical verification task using 15+ parallel spark subagents.
- Updated task 01 in story-operator-architecture-docs to add no-programming-code rule, minimal config snippets only, and expanded config field explanation requirements including PostgreSQL runtime implications.
- Added task 02 in story-operator-architecture-docs for post-rewrite claim verification with strict sequencing (after task 01), claim inventory + matrix generated after rewrite, 15+ spark subagents in parallel, and skeptical evidence standards.

- User clarified config docs should allow full example config dumps, then explain section-by-section with snippets.
- Updated task 01 wording and acceptance criteria: still no programming-language code blocks, but full config example is allowed and expected in config docs before detailed breakdown.

- User asked for unbiased deep thinking on docs structure, with comparison of multiple orderings and pros/cons.
- Re-read current docs/src/SUMMARY.md to ground recommendations in actual current structure.
- Prepared neutral multi-option IA analysis (system-book, runbook-first, Diataxis strict, lifecycle-based, role-split, hybrid) with tradeoff reasoning and operator-focused recommendation.

- User approved plan and provided concrete refinements: Start Here should focus on what/why/how + doc navigation; quick start should be separate before full operator guide; recommended production config profile first; troubleshooting by symptom/error/log; explicit safety case; contributors section much deeper; same book with subchapters/sub-subchapters.
- Updated task 01 accordingly with explicit six-part IA order and revised getting-started pages (`index.md`, `quickstart.md`, `initial-validation.md`).
- Added scope/acceptance requirements for symptom-led troubleshooting, dedicated Safety Case chapter, deep contributor internals coverage, and allowed conceptual re-explanations across sections while keeping canonical facts.

- User asked which meta tasks exist and what they do.
- Read progress log and scanned .ralph/tasks for meta-task markers and do-meta runs.
- Found recurring meta-task file plus pass tasks 1/2/3 in the rust-system-harness story lane.
- Confirmed only one active recurring meta-task (`18-task-recurring...`) and three completed do-meta execution pass tasks (19,20,21).
